reviews.author: RolfMaria- K.
reviews.text: An impressive architectural monument in the far north of Vietnam. Of course, it attracts thousands of tourists, but since I was already there at 8:30 a.m., the crowds were still manageable, and I was able to climb the tower without any problems. I was even allowed to attend the opening ceremony by the state officials. Before entering the platform, there is an official speech by a uniformed, distinguished state official, followed by the playing of the national anthem, which is very moving. Due to the early hour, there were no problems climbing the tower and enjoying the great view. It may be helpful to study the history of the Flangturm in your own language beforehand. In any case, this is a unique sight. The journey there alone is not only an adventure, but also very impressive. It is best to arrive very early to avoid the crowds of tourists.

reviews.author: Stephen Farnan
reviews.text: The entire Ha Giang loop is amazing - would highly recommend it.. particularly with Ha Giang Loop Tours

reviews.author: Leo van Galen
reviews.text: Big red flag!!! ;) Climb up the stairs wasn’t too difficult, great view from the top. Can just about see China too! You can climb right to top where the flag pole is.

reviews.author: Emiel Milner
reviews.text: Pretty cool. Well high up. Would keep going to northern most point too. Cool temple over the hill too

reviews.author: Ollie Johnson
reviews.text: Amazing scenery with a great view of China and Vietnam. Its got toilets and shops if a drink is needed. Visiting is so worth it if you want one of the best views. Just be warned that there are quite a few steps to get up to the top.

reviews.author: Han Nguyen
reviews.text: The flag tower is on northernmost point of Vietnam, located atop a mountain peek and offers a breathtaking views of the surrounding areas.

reviews.author: Anh Karma
reviews.text: One of the most majestic places I have ever been. The road to get there is like a snake. Besides, there are toilets and shops for tourists. 🤩🤩 I am proud to be Vietnamese and I like to climb to the top of Lung Cu Flag Tower because I can see the whole ground below and communicate with foreigners who come there 😁😁

reviews.author: Sandeep Akurathi
reviews.text: Lung Cu Flag Point is slightly off the main route, but it is absolutely worth the detour. The journey itself offers stunning scenic views, making the drive enjoyable and memorable. Once you arrive, you have two options to reach the flag tower, either climb the steps or take a buggy ride for just 30,000 VND. I choose to climb the steps and enjoy the scenic. The panoramic views of northern Vietnam are truly beautiful. It's definitely a must-visit spot.

reviews.author: Panagiotis Goulis
reviews.text: Amazing experience, doing the loop, beautiful place

reviews.author: Tristan Titeux
reviews.text: This flag was huge, you get a nice 360° view from the top.

reviews.author: lisan “Lis”
reviews.text: Very nice climb up the stairs to the flag. You can climb up to where the flag is within the tower as well. From here you can see China that’s only 3km away. We took motorbike ride up from the bottom to the start of the climb as private tour car cannot take us to the entry point.

reviews.author: Suraj Yengde
reviews.text: we rode from male, a place nearby. it is a very well maintained highway. the roads were smooth. once you arrive at the site, you will have to park and climb a steep hill that has nearly 100 steps. be ready to be physically fit. upon arrival, there are another few meter high spiral steps at the flag point. you can see the part of china which is the same as you see in vietnam.

reviews.author: Jack Getz
reviews.text: The stairs are hard work but the view is rewarding...A special place for me.

reviews.author: Sapa 1Day
reviews.text: It's not a beautiful place, but it's a meaningful place. where the first border marker of northern Vietnam was located. The 54m2 flagpole symbolizes 54 ethnic groups and 279 steps to remember February 1979 as the day the Chinese invaded Vietnam and the war lasted 10 years. The lolo ethnic group and the lolo village also have a special culture from bronze drums and dances around the fire. If you are a culture lover. Stay here and find what's real, you won't waste your time.

reviews.author: Belle
reviews.text: Fee: 40,000VND per person Parking for motorcycles: 5,000VND The road to Lung Cu town is well paved. We drove for over an hour from Dong Van on a mix of windy, steep, downhill roads. It's great for motorcycle riders. You climb over a hundred steps to get to the hill with great 360 view of the town and if weather permits, you can see a glimpse of the border and China. To reach the top, you can climb the building about 3 storeys high and youre closer to the flag. We stayed for over an hour. It was raining and foggy but we got clear skies for a few seconds before another fog cloud comes in. Better be picture ready all the time. Check the weather and try not to stay until late like we did. We left at 5pm and it was hard driving with thick fog.

reviews.author: Jose Emilio Aragundi Velez Jeav
reviews.text: The place it’s an amazing view point surrounded by mountains, amazing temples and lovely people. Just be aware that’s sometime they are closed.

reviews.author: Tam Huynh Hong
reviews.text: If you’re on the Ha Giang loop then add this to your stopping points. The view is stunning. One little hiccup was the timing of my arrival here with the flag ceremony. It was around 10 min long, and you have to wait in line until it’s over before you can go around the base or walk the flight of stairs to the top.

reviews.author: Jeroen De Koninck
reviews.text: About 5 minutes to get to the top. Not the most impressive lookout point on the trip but worth the visit. Free toilets.

reviews.author: Fan
reviews.text: This place is nice, but it so overcrowded because all the group Ha Giang Loop tours are stopping here. The view from here is ok but not wow. If you are not on a group tour, go to the Northernmost point instead, it is absolutely magical there and you can see the China border clearly. Ask locals how to get there!

reviews.author: Hảo
reviews.text: Let me tell you about Lung Cu Flag Point. It wasn't exactly what I pictured, but that's not a bad thing. It's definitely a must-see if you're doing the Ha Giang Loop. The scenery is incredible. You climb up this winding road with rice terraces clinging to the mountainsides. It's unlike anything I've ever seen. Then you get to the top, and there it is - this imposing flag tower flying a giant Vietnamese flag. It's a very powerful image, especially knowing the history of the place. Now, here's the thing. The climb up can be a bit brutal, especially on a motorbike. There's a lot of tourist stuff around the base too, which can be a bit of a turn-off. But hey, that comes with the territory at a popular spot. Overall, Lung Cu Flag Point is a worthwhile stop. The views are stunning, and it's a powerful symbol of Vietnamese pride. Just be prepared for a bit of a climb and some crowds.

reviews.author: DANWAY HOLDINGS
reviews.text: Lung Cu flag poles where the top point of Vietnam, close to China border , you may see it from the peak. We went there in Jan , not too crowded, it is windy and cold. Buy ticket at entrance cost vnd 35000 per pax, electric car take you up to gate and if you want to climb to the poles , there is a stairs about 300 steps and go into the pole take another 100 steps to go further to see the panorama view. If the weather is foggy you might not see any things, it is all about your lucks

reviews.author: Tammy Pyne
reviews.text: We caught the buggy up and then walked up quite a few stairs, amazing views from the top.

reviews.author: Tran Viet
reviews.text: ✅ 360-degree views of the Đồng Văn Karst Plateau Geopark, terraced rice fields, ethnic villages, and even Chinese mountains 🥲 839 stone steps

reviews.author: Martin Cavina
reviews.text: This is a must visit if you are in the very north of Vietnam

reviews.author: Eh U
reviews.text: Great view, the stairs are not so bad to climb. Worth it just for the view and the cool air on top.

reviews.author: Catarina Alves
reviews.text: It is a conquer to come if you’re doing the loop but to be honest is just some nice views which you already have if doing the loop, it is fun to drive the rote till here and back but is just a tourist point, the place is full of buses and tours if is very annoying on how people want to take the perfect picture and won’t move from the places, will only move after 500 pictures taken, people are very disrespectful just to take their photos and that results in useless and meaningless cues because of dumb people, also the fee of 40K is quite high just to basically go up some stairs see some views which you already have from other places nearby and to go up the tower…

reviews.author: Heidi Nguyen
reviews.text: Lung Cu, known as "a high forehead of the motherland" where marks the extreme north of Vietnam, is one of the most popular tourist destinations in Ha Giang. This place worths a long hike up with more than 400 steps from the middle of the stairs to the top. I truly enjoyed seeing a marvellous view from the top and took some breathtaking photos. From the top view of the area, you can see 2 big lakes which are called the "eyes of the sacred dragon", according to the historical legend of the Lo Lo ethnic minority. Be careful if you travel to the flag pole by motorbike. The road to get there is not always in good condition.

reviews.author: cj s
reviews.text: Flag pole near the northernmost point of Vietnam. You can see all the way to China from here. If you are this far north it is worth visiting. When I went in 2018 the road there was largely under construction and a little challenging, I believe it should be completed by now, but keep this in mind in case it is not. There are some vendors on site, so you can buy drinks and snacks. Parking is available at the base of the tower. Recommended!

reviews.author: Marie-Joe Chahine
reviews.text: If you're in the region and you have time it's nice, but there are many views along the ha giang loop that are also good, it's not very special. Also when we went the flag wasn't even up. There are some stairs, maybe about 10mins up.

reviews.author: Bao Tin Xay Dung
reviews.text: The flag tower and Lo Lo Chai ancient village at night, viewed from Lung Cu Temple — a once-in-a-lifetime destination
